feed.title: AO3 - Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: The beat of your heart (And the beat of my wings)
entry.summary:
Author: Hubcap_Stealer_YumYum

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 27,537Chapters: 5/?Kudos: 30

Summary: Who knows? Maybe he’d even earn brownie points with the Batman for being able to take care of his precious city quickly.·˖✶𓆩🦇𓆪✶˖·˳.He wanted the Alien out of his city and he wanted him out NOW. Bruce Wayne, also known as the batman, watched over his city from above and violently repelled any intruders. That is, until a man with a blinding smile who can fly just as high as he can shows up, and a little boy who watched his parents die shortly after. TLDR: Bruce has bat wings and they attract the empathetic Kal'El, who helps him be a better person and father.

entry.title: A Family Man
entry.summary:
Author: EvilDime

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,336Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 4

Summary: Clark doesn't know what to make of Bruce Wayne, but he is sufficiently intrigued to follow the man home and into his large bed. He is surprised by the amount of visitors said bed gets in a single night.

entry.title: Proof of Detection
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 39,374Chapters: 7/?Kudos: 48

Summary: The worst has already happened - Recognition, Rejection, devastation. Now Bruce and Clark are forced to work together to solve a mystery that could alter the course of the world, while helping to build the Justice League. Can Bruce live with what he's done? Can Clark forgive the ultimate betrayal? Will the League survive the fallout if they can't?

entry.title: To Be Haunted
entry.summary:
Author: notalegalist

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,392Chapters: 1/7Kudos: 6

Summary: "One Need Not be a Chamber — to be Haunted" Bruce Wayne has always considered himself to be the ultimate sacrificial pawn. Only he can be the one to shoulder the burdens of his city, of his planet, and now, the clandestine organization that he would rather see burn until nothing remained. The Demon's Head has a new face.

entry.title: Forever and evermore (For Eternity)
entry.summary:
Author: starfishingbythehill

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 6,911Chapters: 9/9Kudos: 23

Summary: “Come on Clark, it isn’t even that scary!” Clark’s face reminds him of the statue in his mother’s garden, the one over the fountain. The scrunched nose, the distant eyes, the thin lips. Displeasure and acceptance both. He really doesn’t understand what’s wrong. “We climb higher in the barn.” “It isn’t the same thing !” Clark denies. “I can’t do it Bruce, I’m not allowed to. It would make Ma sad.” “You’re such a wuss.” Bruce rolls his eyes and jumps down the tree. Clark pouts, crossing his arms in front of himself. Bruce rolls his eyes harder. Clark never breaks the rules and he never seems happy about it. Anyway, he won’t even stay mad for long, they’re best friends so it’s okay. “Let’s go see the horses. Tommy is teaching me how to ride.” Clark smiles at him then. See? What did he say, it’s okay. They can’t stay mad at each other for long because they can’t stay away from each other for long. They are best friends after all.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: The Weight of Never Leaving
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 28,282Chapters: 9/9Kudos: 14

Summary: Jason Todd has been dead for six hours and is already at the garden wall. Martha Wayne runs the perimeter. Thomas holds the suppression. The new ghost doesn't wait for orientation — he has three years of field intelligence and a shit list and unfinished business with every name on it. Above them, Bruce Wayne in the cave listening to Morrow's anniversary digging for the first time with his eyes open. Clark Kent in the city, within reach, for now. Alfred Pennyworth with medication on the nightstand and a very specific idea of where he wants to die. The Weight of Never Leaving is the third book of The House We Never Leave. It is the one where the fracture completes, the talon surfaces, a fourteen-year-old with a surveillance file and no survival instincts rings the doorbell at dusk, and Alfred Pennyworth dies in his chosen place. Clark was right. Bruce chooses the house anyway. He stands at the window. He does not run after him. Every heartbeat hurts.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: red, white & boy scout blue
entry.summary:
Author: mitsukiris

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 38,572Chapters: 7/13Kudos: 121

Summary: 'Bruce chuckles, no ounce of amusement in it, “I believe perhaps you should consider switching to water, Kent.” “Should I?” Clark asks, batting his eyelashes at Bruce. “Why? Am I offending you? Sorry I’m not obsessed with you like everyone else. I know that must be confusing for you.” “You know what?” Bruce eyes him up and down. “I think you are.” Clark is frozen. He can’t move. He blinks a few times, slowly trying to digest the words. He can’t. And Bruce? Well, Bruce’s polite smile turns smug and almost a little mean.' or: Pulitzer winning journalist Clark Kent and playboy Bruce Wayne have to pretend to be friends after one sweet incident. What will happen when this fake friendship turns into a real one? What will happen when it turns into something more?

entry.title: The Raven Roosts in the Cornflower Blue
entry.summary:
Author: Aurora2Sunset

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 60,659Chapters: 12/30Kudos: 340

Summary: Someone new moved to Smallville, in the house on the other side of the Kent farm. Clark meets the new kid, Bruce Wayne, and his first impression isn't great. He thinks that Bruce is spoiled and rude and out of place. Maybe that's why he blows up at him. But he still saves him when he's in trouble, that's just the kind of person that Clark is. After a while they develop a tentative kind of friendship over the summer. Bruce isn't like anyone else that Clark knows, and he feels connected to him in all of his out of place-ness. His own feelings for the other take him by surprise, he only hopes that he can stomp them down before he ruins their friendship over them. - Smallville AU where Bruce spends the summers there and befriends Clark long before either of them become superheroes.

entry.title: This ship, that ship
entry.summary:
Author: Anonymous

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 15,821Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 6

Summary: When Superman saves Bruce, Bruce decides to keep his attention on the other man. Purely to learn more about him as a civilian and not as Batman, he tells himself, not for more personal reasons... right? But he does so in the public eye and Clark is faced with all sorts of commentary on the "relationship" between Superman and Bruce Wayne, giving him a super sized headache. What happens when both eventually decide to be honest?

entry.title: Ever Slightly Out of Reach
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 32,800Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 18

Summary: Three years after Superman died in neural drift, the UN Pan-Pacific Defense Committee has finally found language cowardly enough to say what they've always meant: loving Clark Kent makes Bruce Wayne unfit for duty. They think they can regulate grief away through proper documentation. They think love is a security risk that can be managed through committee oversight. They think the strongest drift came from discipline rather than devotion. Bruce Wayne is about to make their institutional cowardice very, very expensive.

entry.title: Boundaries and ways not to cross them
entry.summary:
Author: morallywrong

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 6,174Chapters: 2/8Kudos: 120

Summary: Bruce had known all day what was coming at him. Like watching a car crash in slow motion, he was unable to do anything about the situation.---Bruce was a man of many faces and he took care of whom he showed which side of himself. When the Justice League gives him the ultimatum to reveal himself or leave the League, he leaves. If only Superman, the man he loved, had shown him support... Instead, the other hero couldn't even look him in the eye.

entry.title: Kinæsthesia
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 76,513Chapters: 12/?Kudos: 151

Summary: "You are your own worst enemy."- Martha Kent Clark comes home to a city still wearing his symbol like a wound. Bruce comes home to Clark. It takes both of them longer than it should. Features: an opinionated elderly neighbor, a neighborhood that gives a damn, a word in Kryptonian that doesn't translate cleanly, and two men who are catastrophically bad at being afraid in front of each other. The longer version: After the Fortress, after the city's grief and the shaharreth on every wall, Clark Kent comes back to Metropolis and tries to figure out what home means for someone like him. Bruce Wayne is apparently part of that answer. Clark is working on accepting this. This is a story about the specific courage of choosing someone who will die before you do. About the difference between happiness and contentment. About the people who show up for you in your building hallway at six in the morning and the people who put a transponder in your cape because they can't say I was afraid for you any other way. Martha Kent called it. She usually does.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: The Night is Darkest (Just Before the Dawn)
entry.summary:
Author: m0ld7igma

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 12,697Chapters: 7/?Kudos: 5

Summary: Bruce Wayne is a man holding up a collapsing sky, his hands raw and his mind fracturing under the weight. A crushing, manic despair that feels less like sadness and more like a high-voltage current vibrates just beneath his skin. His brain is becoming a runaway engine, clearing the static of a thousand tragedies at a thousand miles an hour, leaving him constantly on the verge of tearing himself apart just to let the pressure out.

entry.title: all my pasts and futures
entry.summary:
Author: bat_got_your_tongue

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 30,993Chapters: 8/?Kudos: 325

Summary: Bruce gets tossed around the multiverse, only switching to the next universe upon his death. He wakes up in yet another new universe... this time in Clark Kent's bed.

  • background jayroy

entry.title: Five weeks, Three days and Fourteen Hours Without You
entry.summary:
Author: Gotham_Blight

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,624Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 164

Summary: Clark's been off on an intergalactic mission for the Lantern Corps. That means he's been away from Bruce for five weeks, three days, fourteen hours, and thirty-three minutes. (Not that he's been counting) He has a lot of time to make up for, and make up for it he does.
